non_obvious_links.summary: The obfuscation transport factory, VPN obfuscation manager, obfuscated SOCKS5 proxy, FakeTLS transport, TrafficShaper, DomainFrontingTransport, HTTPSteganography, anti-censorship StegoMeshProtocol, anti-censorship SteganographyEngine, anti-censorship TrafficObfuscator, and ShadowsocksTransport now have opt-in EventBus evidence boundaries for local constructor, record-wrap, shape/unshape, HTTP encapsulation, HTTP-looking query packing, stego packet encode/decode/probe, carrier embed/extract/covert-channel generation, local layer application/reversal, timing-delay reads, AEAD encrypt/decrypt/frame, payload-bucket, rotation, local metric, proxy lifecycle, client-session, and relay outcomes. This lets SOCKS/mesh/websocket/VPN callers cite local creation/not-found/failure, TLS-looking record generation/parsing, shape/unshape, HTTP encapsulation, HTTP-looking steganography, StegoMesh mimic/probe outcomes, carrier-capacity/embed/extract outcomes, local XOR/padding/fragmentation/shaping/encoding/timing coordinator outcomes, Shadowsocks-style encryption/decryption, entropy/size selection, start/stop, connect-failure, and relay byte-bucket events with redacted option-shape, parameter, client, target, SNI, domain, target URL, query, key, packet, Geneva action, carrier bytes, generated cover, traffic payload, fragment, timing trace, profile, and payload metadata, while keeping DPI bypass, censorship bypass, remote reachability, packet delivery, provider health, client installation, anonymity, and production traffic use outside the proven claim until external lab or field evidence exists.

Network relay paths are not isolated from economy state: mesh_vpn_bridge, IntegrationSpine, and share_to_earn feed TokenRewards/reward_events, which publish reward relay events into EventBus. Reward events can now carry bounded upstream_evidence event IDs/source agents plus redacted calculation evidence_metadata without copying upstream payloads, so local accounting can cite prior mesh/control evidence and explain packet-rate/referral/simulation inputs without claiming live external settlement. Share-to-Earn also links the latest bounded mesh/control EventBus IDs when present instead of treating the local mesh-stats file as standalone proof; the Ledger smoke path verifies that mesh reward citations preserve upstream EventBus evidence while keeping upstream payload values redacted.

Marketplace API, node heartbeat auto-release, marketplace services, and TokenBridge share the same escrow event helper, so commerce and chain bridge traces use one EventBus vocabulary. The route-only Marketplace API also emits direct maas-marketplace EventBus evidence for status/create/search/cancel paths, separating local API/cache/SQLAlchemy observations from escrow settlement evidence. The helper stores escrow/listing/renter/actor/node/mesh/SPIFFE/DID/wallet identity as hashes plus presence metadata, stores only safe reason slugs or reason hashes, carries redacted request_evidence for marketplace auth scope, role, idempotency, DB readiness, and permission-boundary metadata, carries redacted settlement_evidence for manual API rent/release/refund, cached uptime/source-quality, timeout cleanup, heartbeat decisions, duration, bridge-call result, DB-write, and output-status summaries, TokenBridge links its policy/actuator EventBus IDs into marketplace escrow lifecycle events and each chain-write event now includes duration, source-quality, SafeActuator usage, submitted-transaction state, transaction-hash hash, redacted context, identity hashes, and redacted result summaries, marketplace API, marketplace settlement, and marketplace janitor now merge manual request/cached-telemetry/timeout evidence plus TokenBridge IDs without copying upstream payloads, and heartbeat-driven escrow release uses maas-nodes-heartbeat with maas-telemetry snapshot-write evidence plus a separate DB-backed node_heartbeat observed-state event after local DB commit instead of audit-log-only evidence.

MaaS node heartbeat keeps the legacy route order unchanged but now passes request-scoped EventBus context into the imported maas_telemetry helper and emits its own redacted maas-nodes-heartbeat observed-state event for accepted and denied DB-backed heartbeat paths. The telemetry helper records redacted snapshot/history/topology observed-state evidence with storage fallback, topology ownership checks, read-only/mutation boundaries, and topology response source-quality metadata. Heartbeat escrow auto-release publishes after telemetry export and attaches bounded maas-telemetry snapshot-write event IDs as settlement_evidence, while the node_heartbeat event records DB commit, approval gate, metric-shape counts, telemetry linkage, and optional marketplace event linkage. Analytics telemetry export, topology reads, and commerce release decisions are trace-visible without treating local cached telemetry or API heartbeats as proof of live agent reachability.

Modular MaaS auth endpoints now emit trace-registered redacted EventBus evidence for registration, login, API-key rotation, profile reads, logout, and account deletion. The maas-auth trace filter links trust-plane API actions to local auth-store and AuthService credential/session observations without treating in-memory state as proof of external identity-provider authentication, durable account persistence, downstream key acceptance, billing cleanup, or mesh teardown.

Modular MaaS pilot endpoints are now mounted by the combined MaaS router and emit trace-registered redacted EventBus evidence for pilot signup, status reads, and admin activation. The maas-pilot trace filter links onboarding control actions to local SQLAlchemy user/mesh writes and reads while separating pilot API state from proof of billing setup, onboarding delivery, support-ticket creation, live mesh dataplane reachability, durable infrastructure convergence, or production customer usage.

Modular MaaS node endpoints now emit trace-registered redacted EventBus evidence for local registration, heartbeat telemetry updates, pending-node reads, telemetry reads, approve/revoke mutations, and credential reissue token creation. The events keep node API actions visible under the maas-nodes service without treating local registry/provisioner/telemetry-store state as live dataplane reachability, node identity attestation, SPIFFE authentication, credential installation, or proof that a node executed an approved/reissued credential.

Modular MaaS mesh lifecycle endpoints now emit trace-registered redacted EventBus evidence for deploy, list/status/metrics, scale, terminate, audit-log reads, and MAPE-K event-stream reads. The maas-mesh trace filter connects API control actions and observed-state reads to duration, HTTP return code, provisioner/registry/DB boundaries, bounded result metadata, and control-policy summaries without treating local MaaS state as proof of live dataplane reachability, external node deployment, agent enrollment, durable infrastructure convergence, remediation completion, or join-token consumption.

Modular MaaS BATMAN endpoints now emit trace-registered redacted EventBus evidence for API-level health, metrics, topology, batctl originator/gateway reads, MAPE-K loop controls, manual healing controls, healing-action catalog reads, and mesh-level BATMAN status. The maas-batman trace filter keeps BATMAN API actions visible while separating local helper/cache/batctl/MAPE-K observations from proof of live batman-adv dataplane convergence, kernel forwarding correctness, physical link health, autonomous remediation completion, or production route mutation.

The DB-backed MaaS node heal route now preserves the EventBus evidence chain across the API/control-plane boundary: it passes request-scoped EventBus context into MeshNetworkManager, snapshots mesh-network-manager history before and after healing, and returns only bounded event IDs/counts for verify_node_state and trigger_aggressive_healing. Operators can trace the trust gate used by healing without the API response copying raw node IDs, mesh IDs, route tables, endpoints, or verification payload bodies.

SafeActuator is defined in IntegrationSpine but reused by API, DAO, network, security, self-healing, server, and swarm components. SafeActuatorResult now carries typed redacted evidence metadata across the current parse-error-free adoption inventory: 21/21 known high-risk control files are metadata-aware, 63/63 SafeActuatorResult result-call sites are metadata-covered, and the runtime retention verifier keeps 20 EventBus + 5 result-metadata representative local cases typed, redacted, and fail-closed for production, dataplane, trust-finality, settlement, and customer-traffic claims, including IntegrationSpine's own completed outcome path.
EventBus is not just in-memory pub/sub; it appends events to .agent_coordination/events.log, and the coordination CLI can inspect that history.

Service identity was previously only visible at individual call sites. The redacted status API now links 55 event-producing services to their configured identity sources without exposing identity values.

TUN setup is now treated as local actuator evidence: ioctl create lifecycle plus ip link/ip addr commands publish redacted EventBus traces with hashed interface/address selectors, duration, return codes, timeout/missing-command outcomes, and output hashes instead of silently implying mesh reachability or production forwarding.

The legacy VPN self-healing daemon now leaves evidence before and during local recovery: provider guard decisions, ping/SOCKS/FIN-WAIT-2 checks, heal-disabled/provider-blocked/cooldown gates, stale-connection close attempts, xray process scan/SIGHUP, heal-script execution, and optional Reality rotation are traceable with redacted selectors/output instead of being inferred from logs or treated as proof of successful external recovery.

The VPN watchdog now leaves evidence for both health reads and recovery gates: provider guard decisions, ss TCP-state scans, SOCKS/curl/urllib/socket checks, packet-loss probes, xray pgrep, SIGHUP/SIGTERM gates, stale-connection close attempts, heal orchestration, and check-cycle decisions are traceable with redacted selectors/output instead of being inferred from logs or treated as proof of provider health or remote recovery.

VPN leak protection now separates local resolver/firewall/kill-switch intent from proof of real leak prevention: DNS reads, DoH configuration, iptables firewall/kill-switch commands, DNS/IP/WebRTC leak tests, and status reads publish redacted EventBus evidence with hashed selectors and bounded output metadata, while raw DNS servers, domains, IPs, interface names, command args, browser status payloads, and service identity values stay out of EventBus.

DoH resolution now separates local resolver outcome evidence from proof of real DNS privacy: system-DNS fallback and DoH HTTP attempts publish redacted EventBus evidence with service identity presence, duration, resolver mode, hashed domain/server selectors, HTTP/DNS status, timeout/parser/error outcome classes, bounded attempt counts, and answer counts, while get_last_resolution_evidence exposes only a bounded event reference for downstream layers and raw domains, query URLs, server names/URLs, answer records, response bodies, exceptions, and service identity values stay out of EventBus.

VPN DNS leak tests and VPN leak status reads now link their redacted vpn-leak-protector EventBus summary to bounded doh-resolver event IDs produced during domain resolution. This gives the control spine a cross-layer evidence chain from leak-test verdict or status snapshot to resolver outcome while still keeping raw domains, resolved IPs, DoH query URLs, server identifiers, response payloads, and resolver exceptions outside EventBus.

The /vpn/status API now emits a redacted vpn-api-status-read EventBus event that links the operator-facing TCP/x-ui status read to the latest bounded vpn-leak-protector status evidence reference. This raises VPN status from a standalone online/offline response into the evidence chain while preserving the public response shape and keeping raw VPN server names, interfaces, DNS servers, resolver details, service identities, and raw leak-protector payloads out of EventBus.

The experimental VPN API now turns config generation, local TCP status reads, admin user reads, and stub delete responses into redacted EventBus evidence while explicitly marking bypass_confirmed=false and dataplane_confirmed=false. This keeps generated VLESS/config material, UUIDs, user selectors, server names, emails, admin tokens, and identity values out of EventBus and separates local experimental config/status behavior from censorship-bypass or customer-traffic claims.

Mesh status/peers/routes API responses can now carry bounded Yggdrasil EventBus event IDs plus operator-facing control_policy_evidence: canonical and legacy FastAPI endpoints reuse request EventBus context where available, call the Yggdrasil client with include_evidence, and return only redacted evidence references and the latest shared mesh_metric_evidence_policy summary while raw command output and raw control payloads remain bounded inside EventBus history.
